frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: policy learned from reward over latent multivoxel brain states

frameworks.name: real-time fMRI multivoxel pattern analysis (MVPA) decoded neurofeedback
frameworks.one_line: reward contingencies defined online by decoded multivoxel patterns

frameworks.name: metacognition
frameworks.one_line: implicit metacognitive estimates boost RL over unconscious brain states

frameworks.name: artificial intelligence (high-dimensional policy learning)
frameworks.one_line: humans outperform current AI on dimensionality untangling

core_question: Can humans learn to derive reward-maximizing choices from high-dimensional unconscious neural states?
key_assumptions: task-relevant brain states are below conscious threshold
multivoxel patterns carry the latent information defining reward
implicit metacognition can index unobservable internal states
manipulations: reward contingencies defined in real time by MVPA on fMRI activity
optimal policy made contingent on multidimensional, sub-threshold patterns
key_findings: subjects solved the decision task above chance
performance required implicit metacognition coupled to reinforcement learning
a frontal-striatal circuit supports exploitation of the latent states
interpretations: the brain flexibly untangles high-dimensional tasks via metacognition-augmented RL
this flexibility exceeds current AI on equivalent dimensionality
